records.ety: [L. inhaerens, -entis, p. pr. of inhaerere: cf. F. inhrent. See Inhere.]

defs: Permanently existing in something; inseparably attached or connected; naturally pertaining to; innate; inalienable; as, polarity is an inherent quality of the magnet; the inherent right of men to life, liberty, and protection.
quotes: The sore disease which seems inherent in civilization.
